When arranging shapes, shapes are:

- arranged horizontally and vertically centered in table cells,
- centered horizontally in shapes if the shape is taller than wide, and centered vertically if the shape is wider than tall,
- in text paragraphs centered vertically on the first line and not changed horizontally.

Enter the start and stop position for cutting, separated by ":". If no start position is given, it is set to 0. If no stop position is given, it is set to the end of the text. A negative position means it is calculated from the end of the text.

  Example for "ABCDEF":
  [2:]   = CDEF  Remove the first two characters
  [:2]   = AB    Remove everything after the second character
  [-2:]  = EF    Remove everything up to the second-to-last character
  [2:-2] = CD    Remove 2 characters at the start and end

Enter the text to apply to all shapes. The following placeholders are available:

${text}:		Inserts the existing shape text
$text_len:	Number of characters in the existing text
$counter:	Numbering (continues if the first shape contains a number)
$counter_a/A:	Numbering with lower- or uppercase letters
$counter_i/I:	Numbering with Roman numerals (e.g. xi or XI)
